news.paragraph: Intel is experiencing significant downward pressure following a 10% price crash as the broader semiconductor trade cools. While analysts at Deutsche Bank and Mizuho have recently raised their price targets, they have maintained hold and neutral ratings, respectively. Traders should be mindful of this sector-wide selloff and recent volatility when evaluating contract entries.
headlines: Why Intel Corporation (INTC) Was One of the Top “Blue Chip” Stocks Suddenly on Fire in April
Intel Crashes 10%, AMD Slides 5% as Chip Trade Cools After Parabolic Run
Deutsche Bank Raises Intel Price Target to $100 From $63, Maintains Hold Rating
Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on Intel to $124 From $100, Maintains Neutral Rating
MSFT, DELL And Intel: Which Is The Better Buy
Chip Stocks Are Bleeding Today. The 2018 and 2022 Selloffs Tell You Exactly What Comes Next
